cards.back: A stable state in game theory where no player can improve their outcome by unilaterally changing their strategy, assuming all other players keep their strategies unchanged.
A situation in game theory where no player can improve their payoff without making another player worse off.
A strategy that involves randomizing one's actions to make it difficult for opponents to predict the outcome.
A strategy that is the best choice regardless of what other players do, and it is a good strategy for a player to use in all possible situations.
A concept in game theory that extends the Nash Equilibrium to situations where there are multiple stages or subgames, ensuring that the equilibrium is stable and optimal at each stage.
